content:
ESCALATION REQUEST

Case ID: SS-001
Title: Error -6 during image processing on iX1600
Root Cause: Corrupted ScanSnap Home environment
Steps Performed: SSHomeClean.exe executed, reboot, reinstall as administrator — issue persists.
Request: L2 software review. Possible deeper OS-level component corruption or installer integrity issue.

ESCALATION REQUEST

Case ID: SS-002
Title: iX1600 loses USB and Wi-Fi connection repeatedly after firmware update
Root Cause: Windows component corruption
Steps Performed: SFC and DISM repair sequence performed — issue persists.
Request: L2 Windows component review. Possible deeper system file corruption.

ESCALATION REQUEST

Case ID: SS-003
Title: Unexpected error after firmware update on iX1600
Root Cause: Firmware update did not complete correctly
Steps Performed: Recovery mode via Top Sensor + Empty Arm, standalone firmware updater via USB — issue persists.
Request: L2 firmware review. Hardware-level firmware state may require factory service.

ESCALATION REQUEST

Case ID: SS-004
Title: ScanSnap Home startup crash with tb21 error
Root Cause: Damaged ScanSnap Home installation or configuration data
Steps Performed: Cleanup tool executed, Windows restarted, reinstalled as administrator — issue persists.
Request: L2 software review. Possible OS permission conflict or installation integrity failure.

ESCALATION REQUEST

Case ID: SS-005
Title: Scanner not detected over USB
Root Cause: Stale USB/ScanSnap device registrations
Steps Performed: Old Device Manager entries removed, USB stack rebuilt — issue persists.
Request: L2 USB/driver review. Possible low-level USB controller or driver conflict.

ESCALATION REQUEST

Case ID: SS-006
Title: Vertical streaks in scanned images
Root Cause: Dust or contamination on internal glass/roller area
Steps Performed: Internal glass and roller area cleaned — streaks persist.
Request: Hardware L2 review. Possible CIS module degradation requiring replacement.

ESCALATION REQUEST

Case ID: SS-007
Title: Cloud button remains white after update (iX1600/iX1500)
Root Cause: Cloud token or profile cache issue after update
Steps Performed: Re-logged into ScanSnap Cloud, recreated cloud profile, reconnected Wi-Fi — issue persists.
Request: L2 cloud/network review. Possible backend token synchronization failure.

ESCALATION REQUEST

Case ID: SS-008
Title: ScanSnap Home cannot save scans to OneDrive
Root Cause: Cloud authorization or upload state invalid
Steps Performed: Failed cloud jobs resent, OneDrive account reauthenticated — issue persists.
Request: L2 cloud integration review. Possible OneDrive API permission or connectivity issue.

ESCALATION REQUEST

Case ID: SS-009
Title: iX100 fails to reconnect after router change
Root Cause: Old Wi-Fi settings no longer match new network
Steps Performed: Old Wi-Fi settings removed, wireless setup repeated — issue persists.
Request: L2 network/Wi-Fi review. Possible hardware Wi-Fi module limitation or router incompatibility.

ESCALATION REQUEST

Case ID: SS-010
Title: Device not responding on iX1500 touch panel
Root Cause: Profile was tied to wrong computer or communication path
Steps Performed: Local profile recreated on correct computer, network communication verified — issue persists.
Request: L2 review. Possible firmware or hardware-level touch panel issue.

ESCALATION REQUEST

Case ID: SS-011
Title: Scan button blinking but no scan starts
Root Cause: USB hub or security software interfered with scanner communication
Steps Performed: Direct USB without hub tested, antivirus disabled — issue persists.
Request: L2 driver/USB review. Possible deeper USB communication failure.

ESCALATION REQUEST

Case ID: SS-012
Title: Paper feed skewing on iX1300
Root Cause: Document loaded at incorrect angle
Steps Performed: Documents realigned, Carrier Sheet tested — skewing persists.
Request: Hardware L2 review. Possible mechanical alignment or feed mechanism issue.

ESCALATION REQUEST

Case ID: SS-013
Title: ScanSnap Home cannot uninstall correctly
Root Cause: Leftover installation registry data
Steps Performed: SSHomeClean.exe / Remove Installation Information Tool used — uninstall still failing.
Request: L2 software review. Manual registry cleanup may be required.

ESCALATION REQUEST

Case ID: SS-014
Title: Imported smartphone PDFs cannot be merged in ScanSnap Home
Root Cause: Mobile PDF structure incompatible with ScanSnap Home processing
Steps Performed: Files converted to compatible PDF/JPG and reimported — merge still failing.
Request: L2 compatibility review. Specific PDF variant may require investigation.

ESCALATION REQUEST

Case ID: SS-015
Title: Customer wants automatic saving without preview window
Root Cause: Scan to Folder profile requires confirmation step
Steps Performed: ScanDirect profile configured — workflow still not meeting customer requirement.
Request: Feature/compatibility review for alternative workflow.

ESCALATION REQUEST

Case ID: SS-016
Title: Scanner remains Ready but has no mechanical movement
Root Cause: Mechanism initialization state stuck
Steps Performed: Power-drain reset performed, feed rollers manually rotated — no improvement.
Request: Hardware L2 review. Possible motor or mechanism failure.

ESCALATION REQUEST

Case ID: SS-017
Title: ScanSnap Home unstable after Windows update — errors 0x8007045A/0xc000007b/PFUSSMON/FullTrustNotifier
Root Cause: Corrupted Windows runtime or application environment
Steps Performed: Full cleanup/reinstall, Visual C++ repair, .NET check, SFC/DISM — issue persists.
Request: L2 Windows/runtime review. Deep OS corruption may require Windows repair or reinstall.

ESCALATION REQUEST

Case ID: SS-018
Title: Mobile app scans work but desktop ScanSnap Home does not
Root Cause: Desktop-side ScanSnap Home configuration corrupted
Steps Performed: Desktop scanner registration reset, local configuration rebuilt — issue persists.
Request: L2 software review. OS-level profile or permission conflict suspected.

ESCALATION REQUEST

Case ID: SS-019
Title: Scanner disappears after Windows sleep or hibernate
Root Cause: Windows power-state recovery interrupts scanner communication
Steps Performed: Fast Startup disabled, USB selective suspend disabled — issue persists.
Request: L2 power management/USB review. Possible driver or BIOS power setting conflict.

ESCALATION REQUEST

Case ID: SS-020
Title: Scanner stuck on logo screen after interrupted firmware process (iX1600/iX1500)
Root Cause: Interrupted firmware state prevented normal boot
Steps Performed: Low-level recovery mode via USB, standalone firmware recovery — still stuck.
Request: Hardware/firmware L2 review. Factory service may be required.

ESCALATION REQUEST

Case ID: SS-021
Title: Settings temporarily greyed out and scanner connection intermittent
Root Cause: Unstable ScanSnap Home service or UI communication
Steps Performed: SSHomeClean.exe cleanup, fresh installation — issue persists.
Request: L2 software/service review.

ESCALATION REQUEST

Case ID: SS-022
Title: Scanner connected by USB but ScanSnap Home shows device not connected
Root Cause: Old USB registrations blocked initialization
Steps Performed: Direct USB tested, stale Device Manager entries removed, USB stack rebuilt — issue persists.
Request: L2 USB/driver review.

ESCALATION REQUEST

Case ID: SS-023
Title: iX500 firmware updater fails on newer macOS
Root Cause: Firmware updater compatibility or environment limitation
Steps Performed: Standalone firmware package and compatible workflow used — updater still failing.
Request: L2 macOS compatibility review. Model may have reached supported macOS limit.

ESCALATION REQUEST

Case ID: SS-024
Title: Scanner can scan from device but ScanSnap Home is unusable
Root Cause: Hardware functional but local software environment damaged
Steps Performed: Cleanup/reinstall, local configuration reset — ScanSnap Home still unusable.
Request: L2 software review. Possible deep OS or user profile conflict.

ESCALATION REQUEST

Case ID: SS-025
Title: Cloud profiles stop working after password or account change
Root Cause: Old cloud authentication tokens invalid
Steps Performed: ScanSnap Cloud profiles removed and recreated, reauthenticated — issue persists.
Request: L2 cloud/authentication review. Backend token invalidation may require account-level reset.

ESCALATION REQUEST

Case ID: SS-026
Title: Orange warning symbol on iX1600 without clear error text
Root Cause: Profile destination or cloud communication needs attention
Steps Performed: Profile destination verified, broken cloud profile recreated — warning persists.
Request: L2 cloud/profile review.

ESCALATION REQUEST

Case ID: SS-027
Title: ScanSnap Home crashes during OCR processing
Root Cause: OCR or image-processing workload/component instability
Steps Performed: Image quality reduced, local application environment rebuilt — crashes persist.
Request: L2 OCR/processing component review.

ESCALATION REQUEST

Case ID: SS-028
Title: Scanner disconnects during large batch scans
Root Cause: USB power management or unstable connection
Steps Performed: Direct USB without dock/hub tested, USB power saving disabled — disconnects persist.
Request: L2 USB power/hardware review.

ESCALATION REQUEST

Case ID: SS-029
Title: Wireless setup fails on Windows 11 24H2
Root Cause: Windows location permission blocks wireless setup
Steps Performed: Windows location permission enabled for ScanSnap Home — wireless setup still failing.
Request: L2 Windows 11 24H2 compatibility review.

ESCALATION REQUEST

Case ID: SS-030
Title: Imported files disappear from ScanSnap Home library
Root Cause: External file moves not reflected in ScanSnap Home index
Steps Performed: Folders refreshed, original file locations restored — files still missing.
Request: L2 library/index review.

ESCALATION REQUEST

Case ID: SS-031
Title: ScanSnap Home startup fails after interrupted Windows update
Root Cause: Damaged Windows component store
Steps Performed: SFC and DISM repair, ScanSnap Home reinstalled — startup still failing.
Request: L2 Windows component review. Full Windows repair or reinstall may be needed.

ESCALATION REQUEST

Case ID: SS-032
Title: Repeated unexpected error after every reboot
Root Cause: Broken local user configuration
Steps Performed: AppData/Roaming/PFU configuration removed, profile environment rebuilt — error persists.
Request: L2 user profile/configuration review.

ESCALATION REQUEST

Case ID: SS-033
Title: Wi-Fi setup fails only on one specific router
Root Cause: Router configuration blocking scanner communication
Steps Performed: 2.4GHz, band steering, MAC filtering checked and adjusted — setup still failing.
Request: L2 network/router compatibility review.

ESCALATION REQUEST

Case ID: SS-034
Title: Scanner stuck on Connecting in ScanSnap Home
Root Cause: Broken communication cache or profile state
Steps Performed: Scanner profile removed and re-added, local services restarted — still stuck.
Request: L2 communication/service review.

ESCALATION REQUEST

Case ID: SS-035
Title: Cannot scan to NAS or network folder
Root Cause: Network destination path or permission issue
Steps Performed: Local folder workflow tested, network destination profile rebuilt — NAS scanning still failing.
Request: L2 network/SMB review. Possible SMB protocol or permission conflict.

ESCALATION REQUEST

Case ID: SS-036
Title: No email program available during Scan to Email
Root Cause: Unsupported or missing default desktop mail client
Steps Performed: Default mail application verified, classic Outlook workflow tested — no compatible client found.
Request: L2 compatibility review. Customer may need alternative email workflow.

ESCALATION REQUEST

Case ID: SS-037
Title: Smartphone-generated PDFs cannot be merged in ScanSnap Home
Root Cause: Mobile PDF structure incompatible with ScanSnap Home
Steps Performed: JPG conversion used, files reprocessed — merge still failing for specific PDF variants.
Request: L2 PDF compatibility review.

ESCALATION REQUEST

Case ID: SS-038
Title: Scanner works only after reboot and fails again later
Root Cause: Underlying Windows communication corruption
Steps Performed: Windows repair sequence, fresh driver/software installation — intermittent failure continues.
Request: L2 Windows/driver review. Persistent OS-level communication corruption suspected.

ESCALATION REQUEST

Case ID: SS-039
Title: Firmware update prompt repeats although latest firmware installed
Root Cause: Previous firmware update did not finalize correctly
Steps Performed: Firmware installation repeated via direct USB only — update prompt still repeating.
Request: L2 firmware review. Firmware version register may be corrupted or not updating correctly.

ESCALATION REQUEST

Case ID: SS-040
Title: ScanSnap Home very slow after large library import
Root Cause: Oversized or corrupted index database
Steps Performed: Active folder scope reduced, local database/index refreshed — performance still degraded.
Request: L2 library/database review.

ESCALATION REQUEST

Case ID: SS-041
Title: Scanner visible in Windows but missing in ScanSnap Home
Root Cause: Broken local device registration
Steps Performed: Scanner registration removed and re-added, services restarted — scanner still missing in ScanSnap Home.
Request: L2 software/registration review.

ESCALATION REQUEST

Case ID: SS-042
Title: iX1600 displays white cloud icon and refuses cloud scans
Root Cause: Cloud session/profile cache corrupted
Steps Performed: Logged out of ScanSnap Cloud, old profile removed, scanner re-registered, cloud workflow recreated — white icon persists.
Request: L2 cloud/registration review.

ESCALATION REQUEST

Case ID: SS-043
Title: Random save failures only to OneDrive
Root Cause: Cloud destination integration unstable
Steps Performed: Local folder workflow tested (OK), OneDrive integration rebuilt — failures persist to OneDrive.
Request: L2 OneDrive/cloud integration review.

ESCALATION REQUEST

Case ID: SS-044
Title: Same firmware update offered after successful installation
Root Cause: Firmware installation did not finalize correctly
Steps Performed: Firmware update repeated via direct USB without Wi-Fi — update still re-offered.
Request: L2 firmware finalization review. Firmware version register may not be updating correctly.

ESCALATION REQUEST

Case ID: SS-045
Title: Cannot rename scans automatically as expected
Root Cause: Expected Save As workflow differs from actual profile behavior
Steps Performed: ScanDirect and Scan to Folder profiles explained and adjusted — customer requirement still not met.
Request: Feature review for advanced auto-naming capability.

ESCALATION REQUEST

Case ID: SS-046
Title: Customer thinks hardware defective because scanning stopped completely
Root Cause: Software environment failure
Steps Performed: Scanner tested independently from ScanSnap Home, issue isolated to software — software environment rebuild did not resolve.
Request: L2 software review.

ESCALATION REQUEST

Case ID: SS-047
Title: ScanSnap Home freezes while loading profiles
Root Cause: Corrupted profile configuration
Steps Performed: Corrupted profile configuration removed, profiles recreated manually — freeze persists.
Request: L2 profile/configuration review.

ESCALATION REQUEST

Case ID: SS-048
Title: All pages identified as blank
Root Cause: Blank-page detection too aggressive
Steps Performed: Blank-page detection sensitivity reduced, grayscale settings tested — all pages still detected as blank.
Request: L2 image processing review.

ESCALATION REQUEST

Case ID: SS-049
Title: Wireless scanning fails but USB works
Root Cause: Router or Wi-Fi environment unstable
Steps Performed: Wireless setup reconfigured, router isolation/band steering checked — Wi-Fi scanning still failing.
Request: L2 network/Wi-Fi review.

ESCALATION REQUEST

Case ID: SS-050
Title: Installer says product already installed
Root Cause: Corrupted installation registry entries
Steps Performed: Remove Installation Information Tool executed, system restarted — installer still reporting product as installed.
Request: L2 registry/installation review. Manual registry cleanup may be required.

ESCALATION REQUEST

Case ID: SS-051
Title: Scans cropped or cut off unexpectedly
Root Cause: Automatic size detection or paper alignment issue
Steps Performed: Paper guides adjusted, automatic size detection disabled — cropping still occurring.
Request: L2 image processing/hardware review.

ESCALATION REQUEST

Case ID: SS-052
Title: ScanSnap Home crashes immediately after launch
Root Cause: Damaged runtime dependencies
Steps Performed: Visual C++ runtimes repaired, local application environment rebuilt — crash persists.
Request: L2 runtime/OS dependency review.

ESCALATION REQUEST

Case ID: SS-053
Title: Custom profiles lost after Windows migration
Root Cause: Local profile data not migrated correctly
Steps Performed: Profiles recreated, workflow configuration restored — migration data permanently lost.
Request: L2 migration/profile review for data recovery options.

ESCALATION REQUEST

Case ID: SS-054
Title: Unusual mechanical noise without paper movement
Root Cause: Mechanism initialization state blocked startup movement
Steps Performed: Power-drain reset, feed roller positioning checked — noise and no-movement persist.
Request: Hardware L2 review. Possible motor or feed mechanism failure.

ESCALATION REQUEST

Case ID: SS-055
Title: Mobile app works but desktop application fails
Root Cause: Desktop local environment corrupted
Steps Performed: Desktop scanner registration reset, local ScanSnap Home configuration rebuilt — desktop still failing.
Request: L2 software/OS review.

ESCALATION REQUEST

Case ID: SS-056
Title: Unstable scanner behavior after sleep or hibernate
Root Cause: Windows power management interrupts USB communication
Steps Performed: USB selective suspend disabled, Fast Startup disabled — instability persists.
Request: L2 power management/USB review.

ESCALATION REQUEST

Case ID: SS-057
Title: ScanSnap Home library empty after folder changes
Root Cause: Library references pointed to moved or missing folders
Steps Performed: Monitored folders updated, local database refreshed — library still appears empty.
Request: L2 library/database review.

ESCALATION REQUEST

Case ID: SS-058
Title: Repeated Wi-Fi pairing failures during setup
Root Cause: Mixed-band router steering prevented setup
Steps Performed: 5GHz steering disabled, configured on 2.4GHz — pairing still failing.
Request: L2 Wi-Fi/router compatibility review.

ESCALATION REQUEST

Case ID: SS-059
Title: Orange LED permanently lit after failed startup
Root Cause: Low-level initialization or firmware issue
Steps Performed: Photos/videos collected, service status verified, firmware recovery/service workflow prepared — orange LED persists.
Request: Hardware/firmware L2 review. Factory service may be required.

ESCALATION REQUEST

Case ID: SS-060
Title: ScanSnap Home unstable after importing damaged PDFs
Root Cause: Corrupted imported files destabilized index/database
Steps Performed: Problematic files removed, local index rebuilt — instability persists.
Request: L2 database/index review.

ESCALATION REQUEST

Case ID: SS-061
Title: Scanner works once after reinstall then fails again
Root Cause: Underlying Windows communication corruption persists
Steps Performed: Deeper SFC/DISM repair, remaining PFU cache removed — persistent failure continues.
Request: L2 Windows/OS review. Possible irreparable system file corruption.

ESCALATION REQUEST

Case ID: SS-062
Title: ScanSnap Home shows offline while scanner display shows ready
Root Cause: Broken software-side pairing state
Steps Performed: Stale scanner pairing removed, local scanner registration recreated — offline status persists.
Request: L2 pairing/registration review.

ESCALATION REQUEST

Case ID: SS-063
Title: Cannot save file only on network destinations
Root Cause: Network path or permission instability
Steps Performed: Local save tested (OK), NAS permissions/path stability verified — network save still failing.
Request: L2 network/SMB/permissions review.

ESCALATION REQUEST

Case ID: SS-064
Title: Scans become slower over time
Root Cause: Local indexing/cache buildup
Steps Performed: Temporary files cleared, monitored folders refreshed, local index cache rebuilt — performance still degraded.
Request: L2 performance/index review.

ESCALATION REQUEST

Case ID: SS-065
Title: Wi-Fi setup disappears during pairing
Root Cause: Initial wireless negotiation unstable
Steps Performed: Temporary USB connection, wireless configuration repeated — Wi-Fi setup still disappears.
Request: L2 Wi-Fi hardware/firmware review.

ESCALATION REQUEST

Case ID: SS-066
Title: ScanSnap Home repeatedly requests login
Root Cause: Corrupted account/session configuration
Steps Performed: Stored account configuration removed, local authentication setup recreated — repeated login requests continue.
Request: L2 authentication/account review.

ESCALATION REQUEST

Case ID: SS-067
Title: Scanner endlessly initializing after startup
Root Cause: Startup position or mechanical initialization stuck
Steps Performed: Power-drain reset, feed mechanism movement manually checked — endless initialization persists.
Request: Hardware L2 review. Possible motor, sensor or mechanism failure.

ESCALATION REQUEST

Case ID: SS-068
Title: Scans rotated incorrectly
Root Cause: Automatic orientation detection misinterpreted document layout
Steps Performed: Automatic rotation disabled, manual orientation settings tested — incorrect rotation persists for specific documents.
Request: L2 image processing/OCR review.

ESCALATION REQUEST

Case ID: SS-069
Title: Scanner fails only on one Windows user profile
Root Cause: User-profile-specific ScanSnap data corrupted
Steps Performed: Tested with newly created Windows user profile — new profile also affected.
Request: L2 Windows user profile/OS review.

ESCALATION REQUEST

Case ID: SS-070
Title: Scanner not detected after antivirus update
Root Cause: Security software blocking scanner communication components
Steps Performed: Security software temporarily disabled, local scanner communication recreated — detection still failing with security software active.
Request: L2 security software compatibility review.

ESCALATION REQUEST

Case ID: SS-071
Title: Searchable PDFs cannot be created
Root Cause: OCR configuration or environment corrupted
Steps Performed: OCR settings verified, OCR-related application components rebuilt — searchable PDF creation still failing.
Request: L2 OCR component review.

ESCALATION REQUEST

Case ID: SS-072
Title: Long batch jobs cancel repeatedly
Root Cause: USB power saving interrupts long-running communication
Steps Performed: USB power saving disabled, direct motherboard USB ports tested — batch cancellations persist.
Request: L2 USB/hardware review.

ESCALATION REQUEST

Case ID: SS-073
Title: Application unstable after importing thousands of files
Root Cause: Oversized indexing workload destabilized library
Steps Performed: Monitored folders reduced, database refreshed incrementally — instability persists with large library.
Request: L2 performance/library review.

ESCALATION REQUEST

Case ID: SS-074
Title: Unexpected error only with specific profiles
Root Cause: Affected profile configuration corrupted
Steps Performed: Affected profiles recreated manually from scratch — unexpected error persists with recreated profiles.
Request: L2 profile/configuration review.

ESCALATION REQUEST

Case ID: SS-075
Title: Cloud scans work on phone but fail from touch panel (iX1600/iX1500)
Root Cause: Scanner/profile cloud authorization invalid
Steps Performed: Cloud account reauthenticated, cloud-linked profile recreated — touch panel cloud scans still failing.
Request: L2 cloud/touch panel authorization review.

ESCALATION REQUEST

Case ID: SS-076
Title: JPEG scans work but PDF generation fails
Root Cause: PDF/image-processing component corrupted
Steps Performed: Processing load reduced, PDF-related application components rebuilt — PDF generation still failing.
Request: L2 PDF processing component review.

ESCALATION REQUEST

Case ID: SS-077
Title: Scanner disappears after Windows wake-up
Root Cause: Power-state recovery interrupts communication
Steps Performed: Fast Startup disabled, USB selective suspend disabled — scanner still disappears after wake-up.
Request: L2 power management/USB review.

ESCALATION REQUEST

Case ID: SS-078
Title: Random profile duplication and missing shortcuts
Root Cause: Local configuration data corrupted
Steps Performed: Damaged profile cache removed, profiles recreated manually — duplication and missing shortcuts persist.
Request: L2 profile/configuration review.

ESCALATION REQUEST

Case ID: SS-079
Title: Cannot reconnect after replacing router hardware
Root Cause: Stored network configuration no longer matched router
Steps Performed: Wireless settings reset, full Wi-Fi pairing process repeated — reconnection still failing.
Request: L2 Wi-Fi hardware/network review.

ESCALATION REQUEST

Case ID: SS-080
Title: Completely undetected after interrupted firmware installation (iX1600/iX1500)
Root Cause: Low-level firmware state prevents normal detection
Steps Performed: Model-specific USB firmware recovery workflow executed — scanner still undetected after recovery attempt.
Request: Hardware/firmware L2 review. Factory service likely required.

ESCALATION REQUEST

Case ID: SS-081
Title: Wi-Fi connected but scans never arrive in ScanSnap Home
Root Cause: Network isolation prevents scan delivery
Steps Performed: Scanner registration rebuilt, computer/scanner subnet verified — scans still not arriving.
Request: L2 network/subnet review. Possible firewall or VLAN isolation.

ESCALATION REQUEST

Case ID: SS-082
Title: ScanSnap Home closes during scan processing
Root Cause: Corrupted temporary PFU processing cache
Steps Performed: Temporary PFU cache folders removed, latest ScanSnap Home reinstalled — closes during processing persist.
Request: L2 processing/cache review.

ESCALATION REQUEST

Case ID: SS-083
Title: Preview thumbnails remain blank
Root Cause: Thumbnail/index database corrupted
Steps Performed: Monitored folders refreshed, local content index rebuilt — thumbnails still blank.
Request: L2 thumbnail/index database review.

ESCALATION REQUEST

Case ID: SS-084
Title: Feed rollers rotate briefly but paper not pulled in
Root Cause: Roller seating or initialization state prevents feed start
Steps Performed: Roller seating checked after replacement, initialization reset performed — paper still not feeding.
Request: Hardware L2 review. Possible feed mechanism or roller assembly failure.

ESCALATION REQUEST

Case ID: SS-085
Title: Firmware update disconnects mid-process
Root Cause: Unstable USB communication interrupts firmware transfer
Steps Performed: Update repeated via direct motherboard USB without hubs/extensions — disconnection persists mid-update.
Request: L2 USB/firmware review. Hardware USB controller issue possible.

ESCALATION REQUEST

Case ID: SS-086
Title: Scans washed out or low contrast
Root Cause: Aggressive automatic correction distorts output
Steps Performed: Profile image settings reset, automatic correction disabled — washed out output persists.
Request: L2 image processing/CIS review.

ESCALATION REQUEST

Case ID: SS-087
Title: Scanner detection lost after moving to docking station
Root Cause: Docking station USB instability
Steps Performed: Scanner tested directly on native USB ports (OK) — detection fails only via docking station.
Request: L2 docking station/USB compatibility review.

ESCALATION REQUEST

Case ID: SS-088
Title: Scanner busy although no active scan exists
Root Cause: Background communication session locked
Steps Performed: ScanSnap background services restarted, scanner rebooted — busy status persists.
Request: L2 service/communication review.

ESCALATION REQUEST

Case ID: SS-089
Title: Scanner disappears intermittently on Wi-Fi
Root Cause: Changing IP assignments cause unstable communication
Steps Performed: Fixed DHCP reservation assigned, wireless profile rebuilt — intermittent disconnection continues.
Request: L2 Wi-Fi/network review.

ESCALATION REQUEST

Case ID: SS-090
Title: Single pages scan but batch jobs fail
Root Cause: Profile/image-processing overload destabilizes long jobs
Steps Performed: Processing load reduced, simplified profile tested — batch failures persist.
Request: L2 processing/performance review.

ESCALATION REQUEST

Case ID: SS-091
Title: Scanned PDFs open corrupted or unreadable
Root Cause: Corrupted output configuration generated invalid PDFs
Steps Performed: Alternate save destination tested, affected profile recreated — corrupted PDF output persists.
Request: L2 PDF output/processing review.

ESCALATION REQUEST

Case ID: SS-092
Title: ScanSnap Home freezes when switching profiles
Root Cause: Broken configuration cache caused UI instability
Steps Performed: Damaged profile cache removed, profiles recreated — freeze when switching still occurring.
Request: L2 UI/configuration review.

ESCALATION REQUEST

Case ID: SS-093
Title: Cannot reconnect after Microsoft account credential change
Root Cause: Stored authentication tokens invalid
Steps Performed: Linked cloud/account services reauthenticated, mappings rebuilt — reconnection still failing.
Request: L2 Microsoft account/token review.

ESCALATION REQUEST

Case ID: SS-094
Title: Scanner LED blinks continuously without scanning
Root Cause: Unstable communication prevents scan initialization
Steps Performed: Alternate USB cable tested, power-management restrictions removed — LED continues blinking.
Request: L2 USB/communication review.

ESCALATION REQUEST

Case ID: SS-095
Title: Scans fail only when OCR enabled
Root Cause: OCR processing components corrupted
Steps Performed: OCR disabled temporarily, local OCR environment rebuilt — scan failure with OCR enabled persists.
Request: L2 OCR component review.

ESCALATION REQUEST

Case ID: SS-096
Title: Duplicate scans after interrupted communication
Root Cause: Communication retries triggered repeated processing
Steps Performed: Profile workflow rebuilt, communication state reset — duplicate scans persist.
Request: L2 communication/processing review.

ESCALATION REQUEST

Case ID: SS-097
Title: Cannot save scans after company security policy update
Root Cause: Updated security policy blocks destination access
Steps Performed: Folder permissions verified, unrestricted local save path tested — corporate policy still blocking scan destinations.
Request: L2 review. IT security policy adjustment may be required.

ESCALATION REQUEST

Case ID: SS-098
Title: Scanner startup extremely slow after software update
Root Cause: Legacy cached environment data slows initialization
Steps Performed: Old application cache cleared, scanner registration refreshed — startup still extremely slow.
Request: L2 performance/cache review.

ESCALATION REQUEST

Case ID: SS-099
Title: Scanner works only in Windows Safe Mode
Root Cause: Third-party background software interfering
Steps Performed: Clean boot diagnostics performed, startup conflicts isolated — conflict resolution did not resolve issue.
Request: L2 Windows startup/third-party software review.

ESCALATION REQUEST

Case ID: SS-100
Title: Completely undetected after interrupted firmware installation (iX1600/iX1500)
Root Cause: Low-level firmware recovery required
Steps Performed: Model-specific USB firmware recovery workflow executed — scanner still undetected after recovery.
Request: Hardware/firmware L2 review. Factory service required.
